'''Zebitrope VIII''' was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="EotE" /> located in the [[Zebitrope system/Legends|Zebitrope system]],<ref name="Gamer5" /> a part of the [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]] [[Sector/Legends|sector]] in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="TEA Zebitrope" /> It occupied the eight [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position in its [[Star system/Legends|star system]],<ref name="EotE" /> which was connected to the [[Cadma system/Legends|Cadma system]] via the [[Cadma Conduit]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]].<ref name="TEA Zebitrope" />

Zebitrope VIII was home to a rare [[Unidentified lizard (Zebitrope IV)|species]] of [[Lizard/Legends|lizard]]<ref name="Gamer5" /> that [[Homeworld/Legends|originated]] on<ref name="CSWE Zebitrope">{{CSWECite|III|369|Zebitrope IV}}</ref> the planet [[Zebitrope IV]].<ref name="EotE" /> The lizards had a symbiotic relationship with<ref name="LCFO">''[[Lando Calrissian and the Flamewind of Oseon]]''</ref> a type of [[Unidentified mold|mold]]<ref name="EotE" /> that only grew on the [[Reptile/Legends|reptiles']] backs.<ref name="LCFO" /> The [[Fungus/Legends|mold]] was the only source of the<ref name="CSWE Zebitrope" /> valuable and illegal drug [[lesai]], and as a result, Zebitrope VIII was regularly visited by [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] after they had completed the [[Spice/Legends|spice]]-trafficking route known as the [[Kessel Run/Legends|Kessel Run]].<ref name="Gamer5" />

==Behind the scenes==
Zebitrope VIII was introduced in the [[Wizards of the Coast]] [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game|roleplaying game]] source article "[[A Campaign Guide to the Centrality]]," which was authored by [[Michael Kogge]] and was published in [[Star Wars Gamer 5|''Star Wars Gamer'' 5]]<ref name="Gamer5" /> on [[July 24]], [[2001]].<ref name="GamerDate">{{SWArchive|url=gaming/other/rpg/news20010627.html|text=''Gamer'' #5. A Domain of Evil it is.}}</ref>

"[[Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are]]," a [[1990]] [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game|roleplaying game]] source article published in [[Voyages SF 13|''Voyages SF'' 13]], placed the Zebitrope [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Zebitrope VIII, in the [[Zebitrope sector]]. Since that article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,<ref name="Twinkle">{{VoyagesCite|13|Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are}}</ref> it was considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="UnlicensedStatus">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' subsequently overrode the ''Voyages SF'' 13 placement by establishing that the Zebitrope system was situated in grid square T-8 as part of the Centrality.<ref name="TEA Zebitrope" />
